module.name: browser
module.description: A browser-window mockup (traffic-light dots + URL bar) framing its body — usually a screenshot image. Makes web screenshots look in-context.
module.aiSpec:
Frame content in a BROWSER WINDOW mockup (traffic-light dots + a URL bar). Block module: the body is the window contents — most often a single screenshot `![](@alias)`, but any Markdown works. Use it to present a web page / app screenshot in context. For a plain framed image without chrome use @card; for a generic callout use @bubble.
Body: any Markdown; typically one image.
Parameters: - url (text, default empty): address-bar text. - theme (select: light | dark, default light): light chrome (white) or dark chrome. - maxwidth (number px, default blank = full width): caps the frame width and centers it; blank = fill the column. - shadow (boolean, default true): drop shadow under the window. - radius (number px, default 10): corner rounding of the window (0–30).
Usage: <!-- @browser url: myapp.example.com, theme: dark, maxwidth: 720 --> ![screenshot](@dashboard) <!-- @end -->

param.name: url
param.label: Address bar text
param.desc: Text shown in the URL bar.

param.name: maxwidth
param.label: Max width (px)
param.desc: Caps and centers the frame; blank = full width.

snippet.label: Browser mockup
snippet.description: Browser window frame (URL bar) around a screenshot or content.
